Prunus avium.notes: Early June to early July per guide; Bing and Rainier dominant

extraction_notes: Month-bracket conversions used early/mid/late conventions: early=days 1-10, mid=10-20, late=20-end of month, full month = day 1 to month-end. Non-leap-year DOY values throughout.
CityFruit graphics use month-band rectangles (e.g. 'JUL-SEP', 'AUG-OCT') rather than within-month granularity; full-month spans were used.

Hidden Harvest Ottawa explicitly cites NFFTT Toronto as a source. Where windows overlap (Malus, Prunus domestica, Pyrus, Vitis, Sambucus, Amelanchier), Ottawa values are tagged as Ottawa-localized but matching NFFTT.
NFFTT cherry page does not split sweet vs. sour; mapped to Prunus avium based on Toronto urban-tree dominance. Hidden Harvest does split sweet/sour and is preferred for that distinction.
POP guide uses bare month buckets (no early/mid/late). All POP windows therefore use full-month spans.
POP 'Plums' species not specified; mapped to Prunus domestica per Mid-Atlantic urban-tree convention. POP 'Cherry' is co-listed sweet+sour, not split.
POP grapes mapped to Vitis labrusca (Concord-type) per common Mid-Atlantic backyard varieties; vinifera also possible.
UCANR calendar provides month presence only, not maturity-stage detail. Year-round California crops (lemon, avocado) given full-year span; calendar peak documented in notes.
UCANR plum mapped to Prunus salicina (Japanese) per CA commercial dominance.
Cornell Lake Ontario page (lof.cce.cornell.edu/harvest_storage.php) was fetched but contains no published month-bracket data — it gates that behind paid newsletter subscription. Skipped per brief.

New England Tree Fruit Management Guide (netreefruit.org) covers harvest *practices* but no published month-bracket calendar at the URLs surveyed (apple/peach harvest pages 404 or empty). Skipped per brief.

LifeCycles Project Victoria BC booklet was successfully extracted (38 pages); the 'Determining Fruit Ripeness' section (pp. 31-32) is a ripeness-indicator checklist (color/feel/separation test) only, with no month brackets. Skipped per brief.
Cherry species disambiguation: Hidden Harvest distinguishes sweet (Prunus avium) from sour (Prunus cerasus); NFFTT and POP do not. CityFruit names Bing+Rainier explicitly = Prunus avium.
missing: NETFMG: No month-bracket harvest calendar published; harvest pages either 404 (peaches/harvest, apples/cider-apples/harvest valid but text-only) or empty. NE harvest timing for apples/pears/peaches/plums/apricots/cherries is left to other regional anchors (Toronto for the cooler end of the range, Philadelphia for the warmer end).
Cornell Lake Ontario: harvest_storage.php exists but no month-bracket data on the page; commercial maturity-index data is gated behind paid 'Harvest Maturity Reports' subscription.

LifeCycles Project Victoria BC: PDF extracted cleanly but content is ripeness-indicator checklists, not month brackets. Vancouver Island timing approximated as similar to Seattle metro for cross-reference.
NFFTT serviceberry data not extracted — fetched but page lacked explicit harvest range (used Hidden Harvest Ottawa value for Toronto Amelanchier instead via mid-June to mid-July match).

POP Harvest Guide includes Cornelian cherry, jujube, rose hips, chokeberry, maypop, kiwiberry, akebia, lingonberry, wintergreen, medlar, hawthorn, sunchoke, Che fruit — all dropped because they do not match a Forager species in the prompt's list.
UCANR calendar lists strawberries (skipped per brief instructions on generic Fragaria) and does not list olives, pomegranates, or loquats (so those CA species have no UCANR data here).
